prerequisites: An Azure subscription with an Azure Storage account (general-purpose v2 recommended; Table Storage is also supported on general-purpose v1).
The storage account name (storage_account_name). Storage account names are 3 to 24 characters, lowercase letters and numbers only, and globally unique within Azure.

Permission to view the account access keys: the user must be a Service Administrator or hold an Azure role that includes the Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/listkeys/action permission (for example Owner, Contributor, or Storage Account Key Operator Service Role).
For sovereign or non-public clouds, the correct storage endpoint suffix (storage_endpoint_suffix); the public-cloud default is core.windows.net.

authMethods.name: Shared Key (storage account access key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticates with the storage_access_key secret. When a storage account is created, Azure generates two 512-bit account access keys (key1 and key2) that authorize access to the account's data via Shared Key authorization. Either key works; the two keys exist so you can rotate one while the other stays in use. Copy a key value from the storage account's Access keys blade and supply it as storage_access_key.

setupSteps.title: Identify your storage account name
setupSteps.body: In the Azure portal, open the storage account that holds the tables you want to sync. Its name is your storage_account_name and forms the Table service endpoint https://<storage-account>.table.core.windows.net.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve a storage account access key
setupSteps.body: In the Azure portal, go to the storage account. Under Security + networking, select Access keys, then choose Show keys. Under key1, select the Copy button next to the Key value. Use this value as storage_access_key. You can use either key1 or key2.

setupSteps.title: Set the endpoint suffix if not using the public cloud
setupSteps.body: Leave storage_endpoint_suffix unset to use the default core.windows.net for the Azure public cloud. Set it only when your account lives in a non-public cloud whose standard endpoint uses a different domain suffix.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide storage_account_name and storage_access_key (and storage_endpoint_suffix if applicable) in the connector configuration to authorize access to the account's Table Storage data.
